task_tokens_consumed: Does any 2024-2026 paper propose a deterministic indexed-enumeration approach to MoE token-to-expert assignment via set-partition combinatorics?

hypothesized_clusters.cluster_description: Replaces learned softmax gating with fixed hash functions or deterministic mappings from token features to expert indices.
Inverts routing direction so experts select tokens up to fixed capacity, guaranteeing load balance without auxiliary loss.
Token-to-expert assignment as a balanced transport / matching problem via Sinkhorn or Hungarian algorithms.
expected_paper_signatures: Unified Scaling Laws for Routed LMs
hypothesized_clusters.claimed_coverage: Deterministic balanced assignment via combinatorial optimization

hypothesized_clusters.cluster_name: Combinatorial and Set-Partition Formulations of Routing
hypothesized_clusters.cluster_description: Treats batch-to-expert mapping as partition/combinatorial-design problem with explicit indexing schemes (block designs, Latin squares, set partitions).
hypothesized_clusters.claimed_coverage: Explicit enumeration of partitions of N tokens into E experts

hypothesized_clusters.cluster_description: Routing schemes that drop differentiable gate and use discrete indices, lookup tables, or content-addressable maps.
Making sparse-MoE inference bit-exact reproducible at fixed seed, quantizing/rounding gate decision to deterministic index.
hypothesized_clusters.claimed_coverage: Engineering-side determinism overlapping with S1 scenario

landscape_completeness_claim: These six clusters span the main axes along which a 2024-2026 set-partition-enumeration MoE proposal would have nontrivial prior-art overlap.
